methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Drafter is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Chris Barton and Peter Atkin that has raised $3M+. The valuation analysis draws on a sector peer universe of 9 private firms and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), including robotics specialists such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here, using the $3M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $8M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable; while the Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains seven comparable firms, insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with similar valuation tiers exist to produce a reliable median (fewer than three datapoints meet narrow filters).

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $6M (low) to $9M (high), with a median of $8M. This range rests on a single methodological leg—Stage-Progression—and therefore carries elevated execution risk. Key uncertainties include Drafter's product-market fit trajectory relative to the peer set, the actual timing and size of the Series A round, and whether sector comparables (which span heterogeneous subsegments) provide meaningful anchors for a frontier-stage generalist robotics or manufacturing automation play. Validation would require additional peer data or revenue-based metrics if available.
